revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Bakuya Kofun]][[ja:牧野古墳]]
{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Japan|qq=}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Bakuya Kofun | native_name =牧野古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Bakuya Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Bakuya Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Bakuya Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.55430556|N|135.72416667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|en|Kōryō, Nara|qid=Q1346830|lt=Kōryō, Nara|1=Kōryō, Nara}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|qid=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|circular kofun|qid=Q11394747|1=Round Kofun}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]|qq=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|33|15.45|N|135|43|26.6|E|qq=}}|qq=}}|qq=}} {{nihongo|'''Bakuya Kofun'''|牧野古墳||qq=}} is a [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Umamikita neighborhood of the town of {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|en|Kōryō, Nara|qid=Q1346830|lt= Kōryō|1=Kōryō, Nara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|qid=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}} in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|qid=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1983.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/163361|title=牧野古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024|qq=}}</ref> It is one of the Kofuns that make up the Umami Kofun cluster. ==Overview== The Bakuya Kofun is a {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=}}-style circular Kofun built on the tip of a small ridge in the center of the Umami Hills in western Nara Prefecture. The mound measures 48 to 60 meters in diameter and is 13 meters high. The Kofun is built in three tiers, but only the top tier (the third tier) is completely circular due to the tiering. Although fragments of ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' clay figurines have been found on the outside of the mound, none remain in their original position, and it is assumed that they were taken from other ''kofun'' for the purpose of dressing up the outside of the mound. The burial facility is a double-sided horizontal-entry stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] with an opening facing south. It is one of the largest stone chambers in Nara Prefecture, measuring 17.1 meters in total length. The stone used is huge {{ill|Granite|en|Granite|qid=Q41177|lt=Granite|1=Granite}} monoliths, although the Umami Hills are clay mountains that produce no stone, but the largest stone weighs nearly 60 tons, making it the second largest in Nara Prefecture after the ceiling stone of the [[Ishibutai Kofun]]. Inside the burial chamber were one hollowed-out house-shaped Sarcophagus and one combined house-shaped Sarcophagus (not extant) are placed inside the chamber. Both Sarcophaguss have been extensively damaged by robbers; however in [[archaeological excavation]]s starting in 1983, over 20,000 items of {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|qid=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} were found in the ground around the Sarcophaguss and in the passageways.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404|qq=}}{{in lang|ja|qq=}}</ref> Among the ornaments excavated were gold rings and various beads (gilt bronze gardenia beads, small glass beads, and millet beads), while horse equipment included two sets of gilt bronze-plated stirrups made of iron, a fence with metal rims, a heart-shaped mirror panel, and apricot leaves. Weapons included a silver-plated iron sword and nearly 400 iron arrowheads, and among the containers concentrated in the passageway was a gilt bronze-plated wooden container and 58 pieces of {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} pottery, which indicate that the tomb was built between the second half and the end of the 6th century, during the late Kofun period. It is one of the few Kofuns in the Umami Kofun Group that has a horizontal entry burial chamber. The identity of the buried person is uncertain, but from the size of the mound, the size of the burial chamber, and the abundance of grave goods the most likely theory is that this is the grave of Prince Osasakahikohito, eldest son of [[Emperor Bidatsu]]. Currently, the site has been developed as Bakuya Historical Site Park, and access to the burial chamber is restricted, but it is open to the public for a certain period of the year.<ref name="Shiseki"/> It is approximately three kilometers northeast of {{ill|Kashiba Station|en|Kashiba Station|qid=Q3655907|lt=Kashiba Station|1=Kashiba Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|qid=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Wakayama Line|en|Wakayama Line|qid=Q511543|lt=Wakayama Line|1=Wakayama Line}}. <gallery> Bakuya Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|3D model of the Bakuya Kofun burial chamber Bakuya Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passageway of Bakuya Kofun looking towards entry Bakuya Kofun, sekkan.jpg|Inside the burial chamber of Bakuya Kofun with fragments of Sarcophagus 牧野古墳出土 土器.JPG|Sue pottery excavated from Bakuya tumulus 牧野古墳出土 壺鐙.JPG|Stirrup excavated from Bakuya tumulus </gallery> ==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|qid=Q17050860|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)}} ==References== {{reflist|qq=}} ==External links== {{Commons category-inline|Bakuya Kofun|qq=}} *[https://www.library.pref.nara.jp/nara_2010/0678.html Nara Prefectural Library]{{in lang|ja|qq=}} *[http://www.kashikoken.jp/museum/yamatonoiseki/kofun/2019.23.bakuya.html Kashihara Institute of Archaeology home page]{{in lang|ja|qq=}} *[http://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?frmId=1436 Kōryō Town home page]{{in lang|ja|qq=}}
{{Kofun navbar|qq=}}
